body:
1. Add an /update route -- such that the agent can pull from the latest and update on the target repo 
2. Then also add an scheduled action such that the agent regularly run and check for updates

Stage: automatic-update-route
Parent round: 2

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `orchestrate` -> `delegate_issue` (round 2/10).

agent planner selected child issue delegation: Issue #107 is an umbrella plan across onboarding, updates, and bug-reporting. The latest open PR #114 is ready with no further automated action, so continue from that stack head by adopting the next distinct distribution workstream; #62 is already mostly handled and #27 overlaps #94.

manual orchestrate start on issue; dispatching implement

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777779926627 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUyNjkwMjUxOTA6b3JjaGVzdHJhdGU6OTQ6aW1wbGVtZW50Ojk0OjI -->

**Sepo implementation finished**

- Branch: `agent/implement-issue-94/codex-25269081009`
- Pull request: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/115

Added an explicit `/update` route and `agent/update` label path that run the existing `update-agent` skill, plus a default `agent-update.yml` scheduled/manual workflow that checks for Sepo infrastructure updates on the 1st and 15th and opens a PR only when changes are found. Updated docs and focused coverage; verified with `npm --prefix .agent run build`, focused `node --test` route/workflow tests, and `git diff --check`.

title: Automatic updating on the user side
